intro:
Adopte Un Mec (now Adopte) is a French dating app known for its role reversal concept — women browse men's profiles and initiate contact first. It has strong name recognition in France but remains very hetero-dominant and lacks meaningful LGBTQ+ features.

Embir offers French users a universal alternative with verified profiles, bidirectional orientation filtering for all identities, and free access at launch.

comparisonTable.embir: Core connection features are free for all

sections.title: Beyond the hetero model — a platform for everyone
sections.content: Adopte's model was innovative when launched — flipping the power dynamic in heterosexual dating. But in 2026, it leaves LGBTQ+ users without a real experience. Embir builds bidirectional filtering for all orientations: gay, lesbian, bi, pan, queer, trans — each treated equally in the algorithm.
highlights: 10 orientations — full citizens in the algorithm
Bidirectional filtering works for all orientation combinations
France is a primary launch market for Embir

verdict: Adopte is a solid option for hetero French users who like the role-reversal concept. Embir is the better alternative if you want verified profiles, full LGBTQ+ inclusivity, no subscription, and serious bidirectional matching.
ctaText: Join Embir free — the inclusive French alternative

faq.q: Is Embir available in France like Adopte?
faq.a: Yes. France is one of Embir's three primary launch markets. The founding community is active and growing.

faq.q: Is Embir good for LGBTQ+ users in France?
faq.a: Yes. Embir was built from scratch for all orientations — Adopte's LGBTQ+ support is minimal.

faq.q: Is Embir free unlike Adopte for men?
faq.a: Yes. everything needed to meet someone is free, regardless of gender.
